errors.message: Duplicate/orphaned character: there are two distinct 'Maya' characters — the player character (id: 'player', name: 'Maya Ortiz-Chen') and a separate unused character (id: 'maya', name: 'Maya Reyes'). The 'maya' character is listed in episode.characters but never appears in any scene's characters array or dialogue. Its personality/background/voiceGuidelines text is also near-verbatim duplicated from Priya's character sheet (warm/socially fluent/conflict-avoidant, 'okay so', 'taps table twice', etc.), strongly suggesting Priya's profile was copy-pasted from a leftover 'maya' draft that was never deleted.
errors.expectedValue: Either remove the unused 'maya' character entirely, or rename/repurpose it so it doesn't collide with the player character's name and doesn't duplicate Priya's profile.
errors.actualValue: 'maya' character exists, is unused in all scenes, and duplicates Priya's content almost word-for-word.
errors.fix: Delete the orphaned 'maya' character record from the character roster and episode.characters array. If it was meant to become Priya, confirm Priya's sheet is intentional and remove the stale duplicate.

errors.message: Multiple dialogue entries have an empty string for 'text' with no spoken line, relying solely on emotion/action metadata. If the schema requires non-empty dialogue text, these will fail validation, and even if allowed, they read as incomplete content for a text-driven episode player.
errors.expectedValue: Non-empty 'text' string for every dialogue entry, or a schema-approved alternative field for silent/reaction-only beats (e.g., omit text field or use a dedicated 'reaction' type).
errors.fix: Add minimal text (even a nonverbal transliteration like '(laughs)') or restructure schema to explicitly support text-less reaction beats.

errors.message: Jordan is listed in scene-5a's characters array despite being explicitly described as sitting alone 'across the room' — not physically present at the table with the player, Priya, Tyler, and Chloe.
errors.expectedValue: characters: ["player", "priya", "tyler", "chloe"] (Jordan referenced narratively but not physically present)
errors.fix: Remove 'jordan' from scene-5a.characters, or add a note/field distinguishing 'present' vs 'referenced' characters if the schema needs to track off-screen mentions.

warnings.message: The 'outcomes' array uses loose, human-readable path notation (e.g., 'scene-6a(1/2)', 'scene-priya-(fade/respect)') rather than exact scene/choice IDs, making it unsuitable for any automated path validation or analytics tooling.
warnings.suggestion: Consider storing outcomes as structured arrays of exact scene/choice IDs in addition to (or instead of) the prose-style path summaries, so tooling can programmatically verify reachability.

warnings.message: Jordan's pronouns are inconsistently referenced across content: Jordan's official character sheet uses they/them, and most narration correctly uses 'they/their' (e.g., s6c1-9 'Jordan sets the book down'), but scene-5c/description and scene-7 description use 'they' correctly while a few narrator lines use ambiguous phrasing that could be misread. No outright pronoun errors found, but this is worth a final pass given how easy pronoun slips are with a they/them character alongside heavily gendered secondary characters (Priya/Chloe using she/her).
warnings.suggestion: Do a targeted pronoun-consistency pass specifically on Jordan's lines/narration before final approval.

warnings.message: estimatedPlayTime (13 minutes) is only roughly validated — it approximately matches one full playthrough path's summed scene durations (~13.5 min for the Priya→SmallStand→8a→9a path), but other paths (e.g., Solo Table or Ally Arc paths) sum to different totals (e.g., scene-1+2+3b+4+5b+6b1+7+8a/b+9x ≈ 12–13.5 depending on branch). Since branch lengths vary, a single fixed estimate is an approximation, not a guarantee.
warnings.suggestion: Consider providing a min/max range or per-path estimate instead of a single static number, since branch length varies by 1-2 scenes depending on choices.

warnings.message: Ms. Alvarez is introduced only in scene-7 with no prior setup, and her dialogue implies she's been observing Jordan 'all week' (per her character background) — this context is entirely absent from earlier scenes, so the payoff of her deliberate pairing may read as unmotivated to players who don't have her character sheet.
warnings.suggestion: Consider a brief earlier visual/narrative beat (e.g., Ms. Alvarez glancing at the cafeteria in scene-4) to foreshadow her awareness, though not strictly required for logical completeness.

recommendations: Remove or repurpose the orphaned 'maya' character to eliminate name collision with the player character.
Audit all dialogue entries for empty 'text' fields and either fill them in or formalize a schema pattern for reaction-only beats.
Tighten the characters[] array per scene so it reflects only physically present characters, using narration for off-screen mentions.
Consider converting the prose-style 'outcomes.path' fields into structured ID arrays for automated QA in future episodes.

Overall branching logic, ID uniqueness (aside from the 'maya' collision), trait mechanics, and thematic/educational alignment are strong and internally consistent — this episode is close to ship-ready once the above issues are resolved.
